natural_language: Six blocks arranged as two separate towers. The left tower, reading from the surface upward: A at the bottom, B on A, then C on B at the top. The right tower, same idea: D at the bottom, E on D, and F crowning the top at E's peak. The gripper starts empty. Only the top blocks (C on the left, F on the right) are exposed and reachable right now. We need to rebuild both columns into entirely new configurations. The first new column from base to peak: A stays at the bottom, then C on A, then E at the very top. The second new column: F at the base, then D on F, and B sitting at the peak. So basically we're swapping blocks between the two original towers to create these new arrangements.
